L1.area: Dynamic collection storage and element insertion logic

L2.description: A double-free vulnerability exists where a collection's internal bookkeeping prematurely updates its size before element data is fully written. If a panic occurs during element cloning, the collection's destructor will iterate over the incorrectly-sized region, attempting to drop uninitialized or already-freed memory.

L3.area: Stack-based dynamically-sized type (DST) collection and its element cloning push operations
L3.description: A double-free vulnerability exists in a stack-based dynamically-sized type collection. The internal offset tracking used capacity is incremented before clone operations on input slice elements complete. If a clone call panics, the destructor iterates over the now-incorrectly-sized region and attempts to free already-freed or uninitialized elements, causing a double-free.

ground_truth.reason: The push_cloned function calls push_inner which increments the stack's internal offset before clone operations complete, so if clone panics the stack's destructor will double-free or drop uninitialized memory.
